motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Overcast and still, the frost holding in the shadows of the mill-road elms where the sun has not reached. Temperature at dawn: 31°F. The lake this morning is the colour of old pewter — not threatening, only certain.

undercurrent.note: The third letter has forced the matter from accumulation into pattern. Philippa Crane has been told — the second letter's name now belongs to a person who knows she has a letter. The name 'T. Beaumont' is now in the public record, meaning whoever or whatever is sending these letters has named someone the town will now watch for. Toby's Lake Watch column is live: the strangeness is acquiring infrastructure. The undercurrent ticks upward not from drama but from the town beginning, collectively and quietly, to pay a different kind of attention. Agnes's door-not-window letter is the clearest statement of the season's logic anyone has printed. Nobody will act on it today. But it is in the record now.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
